title: chroot: handle slightly broken seccomp defaults
body: When a seccomp rule includes multiple equality checks for the same argument for a syscall, they can never ALL be satisfied.  Because that's how they're supposed to be treated, libseccomp returns an error when we try to add them as part of the same conditional rule.  Try to detect this exact case, and if we detect it, treat each condition as its own rule.

:pushpin: Commit f4f2c05 has been approved by `rhatdan`

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/1033abccb7f742e6a2b6c53dea4a48123ef66887.1580246217462560000/index.html), [status-travis](https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/builds/643098401?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing 1033abccb7f742e6a2b6c53dea4a48123ef66887 to master...

> I have a hard time reviewing this. Is there any chance we can generalize the problem and get it into `libseccomp-golang`?

Why was it merged without a reply to my concerns above?
I saw this as a separate PR, not necessarily related to Buildah.  I am also wondering if the chroot code is specific to Buildah, and not sure if this is useful to other projects.
FWIW, we're doing this here instead of the seccomp bindings to mirror part of how runc does things in https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/1616.
